name: python3-serial
title: python3-serial
summary: pyserial - module encapsulating access for the serial port
description:
pyserial - module encapsulating access for the serial port
This module encapsulates the access for the serial port. It provides
back-ends for standard Python running on Windows, Linux, BSD (possibly
any POSIX compliant system). The module named "serial" automatically
selects the appropriate back-end.

arguments.description: Option present in package usage line for pyserial-miniterm.
arguments.raw: -miniterm  Option present in package usage line for pyserial-miniterm.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use pyserial-miniterm because pyserial - module encapsulating access for the serial port.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use pyserial-miniterm as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `pyserial-miniterm [options] [args]` as documented upstream for pyserial-miniterm.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair pyserial-miniterm with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `pyserial-miniterm --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

arguments.name: -miniterm
arguments.description: Option present in package usage line for pyserial-miniterm.
